title: Economics receipt record
description: A machine-verifiable receipt: the evidence that a claimed unit of value actually happened. Receipt kinds are CLOSED — winner | defect | savings — and each kind requires its own evidence, so a receipt cannot be satisfied by narrative. The discriminator makes cross-kind contamination unrepresentable: a savings receipt carrying a winner block is rejected, as is a receipt of any kind carrying the blocks of another. Distinct from the SmartOps Planner V2 `receipt_request_schema` (an execution ATTEMPT receipt: started/submitted/failed/skipped) — that records that an action ran; this records that value was verified.

event_id.description: Emitter-generated unique id for this event; the ingestion de-duplication key.

receipt_kind.description: CLOSED receipt kind. Each kind binds a different required evidence block; there is no generic receipt.

x-backend-obligations: PROPOSER != VERIFIER: reject a receipt whose attestation.verifier_id equals attestation.proposer_id, or whose verifier is controlled by the proposing agent. JSON Schema cannot compare two sibling values; this is THE non-gameability rule and it is not proven by this contract.

IMMUTABLE PERSISTENCE: a stored receipt is append-only. Re-submitting the same event_id must replay the stored receipt, never overwrite it; a differing body under the same event_id is a conflict (409), not an update.
TENANT OWNERSHIP: every id in the receipt must be verified against the canonical request context before the receipt unlocks any budget.

METER AUTHORITY: savings.meter_source names an authoritative meter, but the backend must reconcile the claimed amounts against that meter's own records — a client naming 'provider_invoice' has not thereby produced one.
RUN IMMUTABILITY: winner.run_identity.run_immutable_hash must match the backend's stored hash for that run; a winner receipt against a mutated run is void.

WINNER RECEIPT RECONCILIATION: a winner receipt is only a claim until every load-bearing field is reconciled, as a set, against authoritative stored state — none of which a single self-consistent payload can carry. Before the receipt unlocks any budget the backend MUST reconcile: (1) winner.run_identity (run_id + run_immutable_hash) against the IMMUTABLE stored run, rejecting a receipt that names no such run or a run whose stored hash differs; (2) winner.actual_cost_usd against the authoritative cost METER for that run, not the number the receipt asserts; (3) winner.selected_config_hash against the configuration actually selected and measured for that run, so 'what won' cannot drift from what was evaluated; (4) winner.paired_delta against the recorded baseline/candidate paired effect for that run (a paired delta is only meaningful against the two arms the backend actually stored); and (5) winner.promotion / winner.production_follow_up against the recorded promotion evidence (promoted/reverted/production timestamps and any measured production delta). JSON Schema validates each field in isolation and has no second record to compare against, so a STRUCTURALLY valid winner receipt whose cost, delta, config, run identity, or promotion contradicts the stored run is contract-valid and MUST be rejected here (reason winner_receipt_reconciliation_failed). This is a cross-record reconciliation, distinct from the per-field RUN IMMUTABILITY / METER AUTHORITY / INTERVAL ORDERING checks it subsumes for the winner path.
BUDGET UNLOCK: future budget unlocks only from receipts that passed the above checks. An unverified claim unlocks $0.

INTERVAL ORDERING: reject any ConfidenceInterval — winner.paired_delta, production_follow_up.production_delta — where NOT (lower <= estimate <= upper) (reason interval_bounds_inconsistent). The contract bounds the three numbers independently but cannot relate them, so an inverted interval is contract-valid today. On a receipt this is a non-gameability hole, not a typo: budget unlocks on the LOWER bound, so `lower: 0.9, estimate: 0.01, upper: 0.02` submits a losing result as a strongly positive one.
WINDOW ORDERING: reject savings.window where NOT (start < end), and production_follow_up timestamps that precede the promotion they follow. Same limitation, same reason code.

proposer_id.description: Identity of the agent/surface that proposed the spend this receipt justifies.

verifier_id.description: Identity of the party that verified it. MUST NOT be the proposer — enforced by the backend, not by this contract (see x-backend-obligations).

verifier_kind.description: CLOSED set of who may verify. The proposing agent is not a member: an agent cannot validate its own receipt.

Attestation.description: Who proposed the spend and who independently verified the result. Required on every receipt kind.

run_immutable_hash.description: Hash pinning the run's inputs and results. Makes the winner claim checkable against an immutable run rather than a mutable label.

run_identity.description: Immutable identity of the run that produced the winner.

actual_cost_usd.description: Dollars actually spent producing this winner — metered, not estimated.

paired_delta.description: The PAIRED delta between candidate and baseline, with its interval and level. Both the pairing and the interval are required: an unpaired or interval-free 'improvement' is the exact shape of a false promotion.

paired.description: Explicit assertion that the delta is paired (same examples, both arms). A required const rather than an optional free boolean: `false` is unrepresentable AND the field cannot be omitted, so an unpaired winner receipt cannot be submitted either by declaring it or by staying silent about it.

selected_config_hash.description: Hash of the configuration actually selected, so 'what won' cannot drift from what was measured.

status.description: CLOSED promotion status. 'not_promoted' is a legitimate winner receipt: the run produced a supported result the client chose not to deploy, and that distinction is the uptake measurement.

CLOSED status of the delayed production check. 'contradicted' (production reversed the eval result) must be reportable: eval-to-production transfer is a decision-sensitive assumption, and a receipt scheme that can only report success cannot measure it.

then.description: A confirmed or contradicted production result must carry the measurement that says so.

production_follow_up.description: The delayed production confirmation. A measured eval uplift may disappear or reverse in production; this is where that is recorded rather than assumed.

then.description: A promoted or reverted winner MUST carry its production follow-up. Promotion is the moment the eval claim is put into production, and eval-to-production transfer is a decision-sensitive assumption: a receipt that promotes (or reverts) while staying silent about production is the exact shape that can only report success. A follow-up that has not resolved yet is truthful, not absent — production_follow_up.status=scheduled with a due_at (enforced above) says 'promoted, measurement pending' rather than omitting the obligation. 'not_promoted' is handled by the complementary rule below: nothing was put into production, so it does not merely go unrequired — a production follow-up is FORBIDDEN there.

A not_promoted winner MUST NOT carry a production follow-up. Nothing was put into production, so there is nothing to follow up on: a scheduled or confirmed production check on a configuration the client declared never deployed is production-transfer evidence for a transfer that by definition never happened — the exact shape that lets a never-deployed config claim production uptake. Requiring the follow-up for promoted/reverted while merely not-requiring it here would leave that shape representable; forbidding it closes the hole. That the client declined a supported result is itself the uptake measurement, carried by promotion.status alone.

WinnerReceipt.description: Winner receipt: immutable run identity, actual cost, paired delta with interval, selected configuration, promotion status, and the production follow-up (required once the winner is promoted or reverted, and forbidden when it was not_promoted).

example_hash.description: Hash of the offending example. A hash, not the example: the defect is identified and de-duplicated without the content leaving the client.

verdict.description: CLOSED independent verdict. Only 'confirmed_defect' may count toward value; the other three must be reportable or the confirmation rate is unmeasurable and the paid-discovery break-even cannot be checked.

disposed_by.description: The disposition is INDEPENDENT by construction: the flagging agent is not a member of this enum.

disposition.description: The independent disposition of the flagged example.

reviewer_minutes.description: Minutes the independent reviewer actually spent. Feeds the cost-per-confirmed-issue break-even; a discovery claim without review cost is not comparable to human QA.

kind.description: CLOSED remedy kind: what was actually changed or what test now pins the defect.

reference.description: Opaque reference to the correction or the validating test.

correction.description: The correction or the validating test. Required when the verdict is confirmed_defect: a confirmed defect with nothing fixed and no test pinning it has produced no value.

performed.description: The duplicate check is mandatory, so the field cannot say 'false' — it exists to force the check to have happened before the claim counts. Whether the check was real is a backend obligation.

result.description: CLOSED outcome. Without this, the same defect can be re-sold N times.

downstream_metric_change.description: The measured downstream effect of fixing the defect. Signed: fixing a defect can move a metric the wrong way, and that must be reportable.

DefectReceipt.description: Defect receipt: example hash, defect class, independent disposition and verifier, reviewer minutes, the correction or validating test, the duplicate check, and the downstream metric change.

measurement_method.description: Savings are METERED, full stop. The const exists to make an agent-authored estimate unrepresentable: there is no 'estimated' member to select, so an estimate cannot be submitted as a savings receipt at all.

meter_source.description: CLOSED set of AUTHORITATIVE meters. An agent, an SDK-side calculation, and a user assertion are all deliberately absent: they are not meters. Naming a meter is not the same as producing its records — reconciliation is a backend obligation.

window.description: The metering window. start < end is a backend obligation. Required: a savings figure with no window is not comparable to anything.

tokens_saved.description: Signed: a change can cost more tokens than it saves, and a meter that can only report savings is not a meter.

calls_saved.description: Signed, for the same reason as tokens_saved.

latency_saved_ms.description: Signed change in latency, per metered observation.

usd_saved.description: Signed dollars saved over the window, per the named meter.

baseline_reference.description: Opaque reference to the metered baseline the saving is measured against.

SavingsReceipt.description: Savings receipt: metered tokens, calls, latency, or dollars from an authoritative meter. At least one metered quantity is required — a savings receipt that meters nothing is empty.
